title_en: Islamic Sciences Catalogued: Quran and Hadith
title_id: Ilmu-ilmu Islam dalam Kasysyaf: Al-Quran dan Hadis
content_id:
Ilmu-ilmu Al-Quran menempati posisi tertinggi dalam Kasysyaf al-Zunun, sebagaimana layaknya katalog keilmuan Islam di mana setiap disiplin lain pada akhirnya berorientasi kepada pemahaman dan penerapan wahyu ilahi. Cakupan Haji Khalifah terhadap literatur tafsir sangat luas, mendaftarkan ratusan karya tafsir Al-Quran yang membentang lebih dari dua belas abad keilmuan Islam. Entri-entri tersebut berkisar dari tafsir-tafsir klasik besar karya al-Thabari, al-Zamakhsyari, al-Baydhawi, dan Ibn Katsir hingga karya-karya regional dan mazhab-spesifik yang lebih kecil yang mungkin akan tetap tidak dikenal oleh para ulama di luar tempat penyusunannya. Untuk setiap tafsir utama, Haji Khalifah mencatat orientasi metodologis pengarangnya: apakah pendekatannya terutama bersifat naratif (tafsir berbasis riwayat, mengandalkan laporan dari para sahabat dan tabi'in), linguistik (berfokus pada leksikografi dan tata bahasa Arab), teologis (membahas implikasi akidah dari ayat-ayat Al-Quran), atau yuridis (menekankan derivasi hukum dari teks).

Literatur qira'at, yang mencakup berbagai tradisi bacaan kanonik Al-Quran, mendapat perhatian cermat dalam Kasysyaf al-Zunun. Haji Khalifah mendaftarkan karya-karya fundamental: Kitab al-Sab'ah karya Ibn Mujahid, yang menetapkan tujuh bacaan kanonik, beserta karya-karya utama dari setiap generasi berikutnya dari keilmuan qira'at. Beliau mencatat perbedaan penting antara bacaan-bacaan mutawatir, yang ditransmisikan melalui rantai transmisi massal yang tak terputus dan karena itu sepenuhnya berwibawa, dan bacaan-bacaan syadzdah yang lebih langka, yang ditransmisikan melalui rantai yang lebih sempit dan diterima untuk informasi linguistik namun tidak untuk bacaan dalam shalat. Pembedaan ini mencerminkan pendekatan canggih terhadap transmisi tekstual yang umumnya mencirikan keilmuan Islam. Literatur tajwid, yang mencakup aturan-aturan bacaan Al-Quran yang benar, juga dikatalogkan, dengan entri-entri untuk manual-manual praktis utama yang digunakan dalam sistem madrasa dan dalam pengajaran masjid.

Ilmu-ilmu hadis mendapat perlakuan yang sama komprehensifnya. Haji Khalifah memberikan entri-entri terperinci untuk Enam Kitab (Kutub al-Sittah) hadis Sunni kanonik: koleksi Sahih al-Bukhari dan Muslim, empat karya Sunan Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidzi, al-Nasai, dan Ibn Majah. Untuk masing-masing, beliau mendaftarkan syarah-syarah utama, mencatat mana yang paling banyak diajarkan: Fath al-Bari karya Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani untuk Bukhari, Syarh Sahih Muslim karya al-Nawawi, Ma'alim al-Sunan karya al-Khaththabi untuk Abu Dawud, dan lain-lain. Entri untuk Muwaththa Imam Malik mengakui prioritasnya sebagai koleksi hadis besar pertama dan mendaftarkan banyak riwayatnya serta syarah-syarah yang ditulis atasnya, termasuk syarah penting karya Ibn Abd al-Barr. Di luar koleksi-koleksi kanonik, Kasysyaf al-Zunun mengatalogi karya-karya musannaf, karya-karya musnad, ensiklopedia-ensiklopedia hadis besar, dan koleksi-koleksi tematik khusus yang membahas topik-topik hukum atau etika tertentu.

Ilmu-ilmu kritik dan evaluasi hadis mendapat perhatian substansial. Haji Khalifah mendaftarkan karya-karya utama kritik rijal (evaluasi perawi hadis), termasuk kamus-kamus biografi besar karya Ibn Abi Hatim, Tahdzib al-Kamal karya al-Mizzi, Mizan al-I'tidal karya al-Dzahabi, serta Tahdzib al-Tahdzib dan Taqrib al-Tahdzib karya Ibn Hajar. Beliau juga mencakup karya-karya teoritis metodologi hadis ('ulum al-hadis), termasuk Muqaddimah Ibn al-Shalah yang fundamental, Taqrib al-Nawawi, dan banyak ringkasan serta syarah kemudian dari teks-teks ini. Luasnya cakupan ini mencerminkan sentralitas ilmu-ilmu hadis bagi seluruh tradisi hukum dan teologis Islam: tanpa pengetahuan yang andal tentang perawi mana yang dapat dipercaya dan riwayat mana yang benar-benar ditransmisikan, ilmu-ilmu lain tidak dapat berfungsi dengan baik. Katalog Haji Khalifah tentang literatur ini sendiri merupakan panduan tak ternilai tentang tradisi kritik hadis.
content_en:
The Quranic sciences occupy a position of supreme importance in Kashf al-Zunun, as befits a catalog of Islamic scholarship in which every other discipline is ultimately oriented toward the understanding and application of divine revelation. Hajji Khalifa's coverage of tafsir literature is extensive, listing hundreds of works of Quranic commentary spanning more than twelve centuries of Islamic scholarship. The entries range from the great classical commentaries of al-Tabari, al-Zamakhshari, al-Baydawi, and Ibn Kathir to smaller regional and school-specific works that might otherwise have remained unknown to scholars outside their place of composition. For each major tafsir, Hajji Khalifa notes the author's methodological orientation: whether his approach is primarily narrative (riwaya-based tafsir, relying on reports from the Companions and Successors), linguistic (focusing on Arabic lexicography and grammar), theological (addressing creedal implications of Quranic verses), or juristic (emphasizing legal derivation from the text).

The qira'at literature, covering the multiple canonical recitation traditions of the Quran, receives careful coverage in the Kashf al-Zunun. Hajji Khalifa lists the foundational works: the Kitab al-Sab'a of Ibn Mujahid, which established the seven canonical readings, along with the major works of each subsequent generation of qira'at scholarship. He notes the important distinction between the mutawatir readings, transmitted through unbroken mass chains of transmission and therefore fully authoritative, and the rarer shadhdh readings, transmitted through narrower chains and accepted for linguistic information but not for recitation in prayer. This distinction reflects the sophisticated approach to textual transmission that characterizes Islamic scholarship generally. The tajwid literature, covering the rules of correct Quranic recitation, is also catalogued, with entries for the major practical manuals used in the madrasa system and in mosque instruction.

The hadith sciences receive equally comprehensive treatment. Hajji Khalifa provides detailed entries for the Six Books (Kutub al-Sitta) of canonical Sunni hadith: the Sahih collections of al-Bukhari and Muslim, the four Sunan works of Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasai, and Ibn Majah. For each he lists the major commentaries, noting which have been most widely taught: Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani's Fath al-Bari on al-Bukhari, al-Nawawi's Sharh Sahih Muslim, al-Khattabi's Maalim al-Sunan on Abu Dawud, and others. The entry for the Muwatta of Imam Malik acknowledges its priority as the earliest major hadith collection and lists its numerous recensions and the commentaries written on them, including the important commentary of Ibn Abd al-Barr. Beyond the canonical collections, the Kashf al-Zunun catalogs the musannaf works, the musnad works, the major hadith encyclopedias, and the specialized thematic collections that addressed particular legal or ethical topics.

The sciences of hadith criticism and evaluation receive substantial attention. Hajji Khalifa lists the major works of rijal criticism (evaluation of hadith narrators), including the great biographical dictionaries of Ibn Abi Hatim, al-Mizzi's Tahdhib al-Kamal, al-Dhahabi's Mizan al-Itidal, and Ibn Hajar's Tahdhib al-Tahdhib and Taqrib al-Tahdhib. He also covers the theoretical works of hadith methodology ('ulum al-hadith), including Ibn al-Salah's foundational Muqaddima, al-Nawawi's Taqrib, and the many later condensations and commentaries on these texts. The breadth of this coverage reflects the centrality of hadith sciences to the entire Islamic legal and theological tradition: without reliable knowledge of which narrators are trustworthy and which traditions are authentically transmitted, the other sciences cannot function properly. Hajji Khalifa's catalog of this literature is itself an invaluable guide to the hadith critical tradition.
content_ar: The القرآني sciences occupy a position of supreme importance in Kashf al-Zunun, as befits a catalog of Islamic scholarship in which every other discipline is ultimately oriented toward the الفهم and application of divine الوحي. Hajji Khalifa's coverage of التفسير literature is extensive, listing hundreds of works of القرآني commentary spanning more than twelve centuries of Islamic scholarship. The entries range from the great الكلاسيكي commentaries of al-Tabari, al-Zamakhshari, al-Baydawi, and Ibn Kathir to smaller regional and school-specific works that might otherwise have remained unknown to العلماء outside their place of composition. For each major التفسير, Hajji Khalifa notes the author's methodological orientation: whether his approach is primarily narrative (riwaya-based التفسير, relying on reports from the الصحابة and التابعين), linguistic (focusing on Arabic lexicography and grammar), theological (addressing creedal implications of القرآني الآيات), or juristic (emphasizing legal derivation from the text). The qira'at literature, covering the multiple canonical التلاوة traditions of the القرآن الكريم, receives careful coverage in the Kashf al-Zunun. Hajji Khalifa lists the foundational works: the Kitab al-Sab'a of Ibn Mujahid, which established the seven canonical readings, along with the major works of each subsequent generation of qira'at scholarship. He notes the المهم distinction between the mutawatir readings, transmitted through unbroken mass chains of transmission and لذلك fully المعتمد, and the rarer shadhdh readings, transmitted through narrower chains and accepted for linguistic information but not for التلاوة in الصلاة. This distinction reflects the sophisticated approach to textual transmission that characterizes Islamic scholarship generally. The tajwid literature, covering the rules of correct القرآني التلاوة, is أيضاً catalogued, with entries for the major practical manuals used in the madrasa system and in المسجد instruction. The الحديث sciences receive equally comprehensive treatment. Hajji Khalifa provides detailed entries for the Six الكتب (Kutub al-Sitta) of canonical Sunni الحديث: the صحيح collections of البخاري and مسلم, the four Sunan works of أبو داود, al-الترمذي, al-النسائي, and ابن ماجه. For each he lists the major commentaries, noting which have been most widely taught: Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani's Fath al-Bari on البخاري, النووي's Sharh صحيح مسلم, al-Khattabi's Maalim al-Sunan on أبو داود, and others. The entry for the الموطأ of الإمام مالك acknowledges its priority as the earliest major الحديث collection and lists its numerous recensions and the commentaries written on them, بما في ذلك the المهم commentary of Ibn Abd al-Barr. Beyond the canonical collections, the Kashf al-Zunun catalogs the musannaf works, the musnad works, the major الحديث encyclopedias, and the specialized thematic collections that addressed particular legal or ethical topics.</p
